posts.text: Wiles is the first woman to ever serve as White House chief of staff. She noted in her statement that nearly 1 in 8 women get a breast cancer diagnosis each year and still continue to work. She said POTUS has been supportive of her. nytimes.com/2026/03/16/us/po…

Susie Wiles, in a statement to the NYT, says her breast cancer diagnosis was made in the early stages. She will start a treatment court of several weeks in the DC area, and will remain on as chief of staff without plans for a leave. nytimes.com/2026/03/16/us/po…
“There was no way this person ran headfirst into a wall.” abc7ny.com/post/ice-minneapo…
Trump signed the pardon of Juan Orlando Hernandez last night. @WRashbaum @Jonesieman me nytimes.com/2025/12/02/us/po…
NEW: Trump and Maduro spoke last week, discussing a possible meeting in the US, although there are currently no plans to. @AKurmanaev me nytimes.com/2025/11/28/us/po…

The Trump administration is preparing tariff rollbacks on goods from countries beyond those that have reached trade agreements with the US in effort to lower prices @AnaSwanson @tylerpager me nytimes.com/2025/11/13/us/po…
